Tests if the `test` function returns a function type in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code defines a function [`test`](<#09-006test>) that returns another function [`internal`](<#09-006internal>). The [`internal`](<#09-006internal>) function, when called, returns the integer value `100`. The variable `r` is assigned the result of calling [`test`](<#09-006test>), which is the [`internal`](<#09-006internal>) function itself. An assertion checks that the type of `r` is a function, ensuring that [`test`](<#09-006test>) correctly returns a function. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily demonstrating the concept of returning a function from another function.
